pages.title: Wild to Domestic
pages.text: Every farm animal has a $(l)tameness$() level from 0 to 100. Freshly spawned animals are $(c)Wild$() — they flee from players and have limited productivity.

pages.title: Stages
pages.text:
$(6)Wild$() (0–25): Flees players. Maximum 50% productivity.

$(e)Adjusting$() (26–60): Getting used to people. Maximum 75% productivity.

$(a)Domestic$() (61–100): Trusts the player. Full productivity and can breed any season.

pages.title: Gaining Tameness
pages.text: Keep animals well-fed and stress-free — tameness rises slowly each tick interval. Being near a domestic animal of the same species speeds the process. $(c)Hitting an animal$() costs 20 tameness and triggers panic. Neglect causes slow decay.

pages.title: Herd Behaviour
pages.text: Animals feel safer in small groups — 1 to $(l)crowding limit$() animals suppresses mild stress. Panic spreads through a herd. The calmest, most domestic animal in a group acts as a leader others will follow.
